updated = | source = }} '''John Kahukura Raymond Timu''' (born 8 May 1969) is a New Zealand former [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s who achieved international selection for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in both rugby codes, appearing in 26 tests for the [[All Blacks]] in [[rugby union|union]] and nine for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|Kiwis]] in [[rugby league|league]]. Timu's usual position was {{rlp|ce}} in league and in union he would play at full-back or on the wing. ==Rugby union== === High school === John Timu attended Lindisfarne College in Hastings and played rugby for their first XV where he scored 92 tries in 55 games between 1985 and 1987.{{Cite web|title=Lindisfarne College {{!}} CNI Rugby|url=http://www.cnirugby.co.nz/schools/lindisfarne|access-date=2021-04-12|website=www.cnirugby.co.nz|language=en}} After finishing school, he moved to Dunedin to attend the University of Otago.{{Cite web|title=100 Greatest All Blacks: John Timu|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/100-greatest-all-blacks-john-timu/KZ25OAZW6MT2XUZHHC32DS6AT4/|access-date=2021-04-12|website=[[The New Zealand Herald]] |language=en-NZ}} === Otago === John Timu played representative rugby union for [[Otago Rugby Football Union|Otago]], including playing for the 1991 team that won the first division championship.{{Cite web|date=2016-09-09|title=When they reigned: Where are they now?|url=https://www.odt.co.nz/sport/rugby/otago/when-they-reigned-0|access-date=2021-04-12|website=[[Otago Daily Times]] |language=en}} He also played in the 1993 Otago team that defeated the [[British & Irish Lions|British and Irish Lions]]. He scored one try in the 37-24 result.{{Cite web|date=2017-06-12|title=Lions tour: How Otago's famous win over the British and Irish Lions revolutionised rugby|url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/rugby/international/93590405/lions-tour-how-otagos-famous-win-over-the-british-and-irish-lions-revolutionised-rugby|access-date=2021-04-12|website=[[Stuff (website)|Stuff]] |language=en}}{{Cite web|date=2011-10-23|title=Rugby Union: Mauled Lions hurt in all manner of ways: Battered|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-union-mauled-lions-hurt-in-all-manner-of-ways-battered-tourists-see-forward-flaws-exposed-a-week-before-first-test-against-the-all-blacks-as-otago-open-up-defence-with-five-tries-1490205.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220613/https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-union-mauled-lions-hurt-in-all-manner-of-ways-battered-tourists-see-forward-flaws-exposed-a-week-before-first-test-against-the-all-blacks-as-otago-open-up-defence-with-five-tries-1490205.html |archive-date=13 June 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live|access-date=2021-04-13|website=The Independent|language=en}} John Timu was part of a backline for Otago that included [[Paul Cooke (rugby union, born 1967)|Paul Cooke]], [[Marc Ellis (rugby)|Marc Ellis]], [[Jeff Wilson (sportsman)|Jeff Wilson]] and John Leslie. === New Zealand Maori === John Timu played for the [[Māori All Blacks|New Zealand Maori rugby team]] from 1988 to 1994 scoring seven tries for them.{{Cite web|title=John Kahukura Raymond Timu|url=https://www.rugbyhistory.co.nz/player/john-k-r-timu|access-date=12 April 2021|website=New Zealand Rugby History}} === All Blacks === John Timu was first selected for the All Blacks on their 1989 tour to Europe to replace an injured John Kirwan. He played for New Zealand in the [[1991 Rugby World Cup]] scoring two tries at fullback against a very physical Canadian side in the quarterfinals.{{Cite web|title=100 Greatest All Blacks: John Timu|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/100-greatest-all-blacks-john-timu/KZ25OAZW6MT2XUZHHC32DS6AT4/|access-date=2021-04-12|website=[[The New Zealand Herald]] |language=en-NZ}} Also in 1991 Timu was awarded the [[Tom French Cup]] as Māori player of the year. In the [[1992 South Africa vs New Zealand rugby union match]] he played for the All Blacks, scoring a try. He was then selected to go on the [[1993 New Zealand rugby union tour of Britain]]. ==Rugby league== John Timu commenced his professional [[rugby league]] career in 1995 with the newly re-branded [[Sydney Bulldogs]] club. In his first season, he was selected to play for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand national team]] in the second and third matches of the [[1995 Trans-Tasman Test series]]. Timu played in the centres for the Bulldogs in their victory in the [[1995 Winfield Cup]] Grand Final. He was selected for New Zealand's [[1995 Rugby League World Cup squads|1995 Rugby League World Cup squad]] but didn't play any games due to injury.{{cite web|last1=Whiticker|first1=Alan|title=John Timu|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/john-timu/summary.html|website=rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Shawn Dollin, Andrew Ferguson and Bill Bates|access-date=28 August 2016}} During the [[1996 Great Britain Lions tour]] Timu was selected to play for New Zealand at centre in all three [[Test match (rugby league)|Test matches]], scoring two tries in the first. Timu was selected to play for New Zealand at centre in the [[1997 Anzac Test]]. After the [[1997 Super League (Australia) season|1997 Super League season]] he left the Bulldogs to play in England. Timu played for the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] at {{rlp|ce}} in the [[1999 Challenge Cup]] Final. There were rumours that after finishing his contract with the London Broncos, John Timu would return to play rugby for Otago but this did not happen.{{Cite web|title=Rugby: Return of Wilson rumours are just that|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/rugby-return-of-wilson-rumours-are-just-that/6ASXUAUJYYDECO4QD6IEX3SOC4/|access-date=2021-04-12|website=[[The New Zealand Herald]] |language=en-NZ}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *{{All Blacks|id=891|name=John Timu}} *[https://web.archive.org/web/20071014082411/http://www.bulldogs.com.au/main.php?page=player&id=595 Canterbury Bulldogs profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20080423045007/http://stats.rleague.com/rl/scorers/players/John_Timu.html NRL points] *[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.com/players/John_Timu.html Rugby League Project stats] *[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/916748.stm Timu to quit rugby league] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20081014042812/http://www.rugbymuseum.co.nz/ABProfilee.asp?level1=All_Blacks&Level2=ABC&IDID=891 John Timu at rugbymuseum.co.nz] {{s-start}} {{s-ach|aw}} {{s-bef| before = [[Steve McDowall]]}} {{s-ttl| title = [[Tom French Cup|Tom French Memorial
